question: Which 2026 light rail transit line in Toronto, Ontario opened on February 8, 2026 with 25 stations along Eglinton Avenue, and uses standard gauge instead of the broad gauge used by the city's older heavy rail subway lines?
expected_behavior: Line 5 Eglinton (Eglinton Crosstown LRT / 'the Crosstown'). Operated by the Toronto Transit Commission, opened February 8, 2026, 19 km long, 25 stations, standard gauge.

question: Which 2026 metro extension to the Mumbai Metro network opened to commercial service on April 8, 2026, extending the Red Line northward into the Mira-Bhayandar region with three additional stations from Dahisar (East) to Kashigaon?
expected_behavior: Red Line (Mumbai Metro) — Phase 1 of Line 9 extension. Commercial services began April 8, 2026 (after April 7, 2026 inauguration). The Red Line as a whole spans 31.045 km with 22 stations and connects Mira Bhayandar to Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj International Airport.

question: Which Birmingham railway line, used primarily by freight trains since the 1940s, reopened on April 7, 2026 to local passenger services after a break of over 80 years, with three new/reopened stations named Moseley Village, Kings Heath, and Pineapple Road?
expected_behavior: Camp Hill line. Local passenger services ended in 1941 (confirmed permanently closed 1946); reopened to passengers on April 7, 2026 after ~84 years. Three reopened/new stations: Moseley Village, Kings Heath, Pineapple Road. ELR designation: St Andrews Junction to Kings Norton line.

question: Which 2026 commuter rail branch of the South Shore Line in Lake County, Indiana opened on March 31, 2026, branching south from Hammond Gateway station to serve the municipalities of Hammond, Munster, and Dyer?
expected_behavior: Monon Corridor (called the West Lake Corridor during planning/construction). Opened March 31, 2026. Operated by NICTD (Northern Indiana Commuter Transportation District). Project cost ~$945 million.

question: Which 2026 tramway line in Switzerland is the country's first tramway built using standard gauge (rather than the metric gauge used by other Swiss tramways), uses Stadler Tramlink trams manufactured in Valencia, Spain, and marks the return of streetcars to its city after the previous tram system closed in 1964?
expected_behavior: Lausanne tramway, Line T1 — opens late 2026 (Phase 1, 4.6 km, between Lausanne-Flon and Renens stations, 10 stations). First Swiss tramway with standard gauge. Fleet of 10 Stadler Tramlink trams, constructed in Valencia, Spain. The previous Lausanne tram network closed in 1964 (60+ years prior).

question: Which 2026 monorail line in São Paulo, Brazil — originally one of the transportation projects supporting a 2014 international football tournament — opened in 2026 after delays of more than a decade, has 14 stations including a branch to a major airport, and is the first line in its metro system to feature a branch?
expected_behavior: Line 17 (Gold) / Linha 17–Ouro of the São Paulo Metro. Originally a 2014 FIFA World Cup transportation project. 14 stations from São Paulo-Morumbi to Jabaquara, with a branch to Congonhas station (São Paulo/Congonhas Airport). First SP Metro line to have a branch. Pushed from 2013 original opening to June 2026.

question: Which suburban rapid transit line in mainland China, opened on April 22, 2026, became the first inter-provincial subway in its host city and the second in mainland China overall (after Shanghai Metro Line 11 connecting Shanghai and Suzhou)? Tie-breaker: the first subway line in mainland China to be jointly constructed, managed, and operated by two provinces.
expected_behavior: Line S2 of the Nanjing Metro. Officially opened at 10:00 on April 22, 2026. First inter-provincial subway in Nanjing; second in mainland China after Shanghai Metro Line 11 (Shanghai–Suzhou). First in mainland China jointly constructed/managed/operated by two provinces (Jiangsu and Anhui). Connects Nanjing and Ma'anshan.

question: Which 2026 rapid transit line in Ningbo, Zhejiang province, opened on January 16, 2026, has 23 stations all underground, spans 39.6 km, and was built with a total investment of 31.53 billion RMB?
expected_behavior: Line 6 of Ningbo Rail Transit. Opened January 16, 2026; 23 stations all underground; 39.6 km long; 31.53 billion RMB total investment. Construction started December 28, 2022.

question: Which 2026 people-mover line, approximately 2.6 km (1.6 mi) long with 4 stations, connects a São Paulo international airport to a nearby CPTM railway station, and opened to airport employees on December 3, 2025 before opening to the public on February 20, 2026?
expected_behavior: GRU Airport People Mover at São Paulo/Guarulhos International Airport. 2.6 km, 4 stations (Aeroporto-Guarulhos w/ Line 13-Jade connection + 3 terminals). Opened to airport employees December 3, 2025; to the public February 20, 2026. Built using Aerom (Aeromovel) technology.

question: Which Chinese 2026 high-speed railway line in Zhejiang province, operated by China Railway Shanghai Group, is 64.5 km long with a maximum speed of 250 km/h, has approximately 32% of its length in tunnels, and serves four stations between Jinhua and Jiande?
expected_behavior: Jinhua–Jiande high-speed railway (Jinjian railway / 金建铁路). Opened February 14, 2026. 64.5 km, max speed 250 km/h, ~32% in tunnels. Four stations: Jinhua, Lanxi East, Dayang, Jiande. Construction began January 7, 2020.

question: Among the 2026-opened railway lines documented on Wikipedia (including LRT, metro, monorail, tramway, commuter, and high-speed lines), which has the longest stated route length, and what is that length? Tie-breaker: the line whose entire route is underground.
expected_behavior: Line 6 (Ningbo Rail Transit) at 39.6 km, with all 23 stations underground (satisfying the tie-breaker). The Red Line (Mumbai Metro) is 31.045 km (mostly elevated, only one underground station). Line 5 Eglinton is 19 km (only the central section is tunnelled). Jinhua–Jiande is 64.5 km — but a high-speed intercity line, not a 'railway-line opened in 2026' metro, and only ~32% is in tunnels (not entirely underground). Note: among urban rail-transit lines opened in 2026 with the entire route underground, Ningbo Line 6 wins the tie-breaker; if the question is read strictly by length alone, Jinhua–Jiande HSR (64.5 km) is longest, but it does not satisfy the underground tie-breaker.

question: Among the 2026-opened railway lines documented on Wikipedia, which one has the earliest 2026 opening date, and what was that date? Tie-breaker: the line opened in mainland China.
expected_behavior: Line 6 (Ningbo Rail Transit) opened January 16, 2026 — earliest 2026 opening among the lines listed. The tie-breaker (mainland China) is satisfied. Line 5 Eglinton opened February 8, 2026; Jinhua–Jiande HSR opened February 14, 2026; GRU Airport People Mover opened to public February 20, 2026.

question: Among the 2026-opened railway lines documented on Wikipedia that explicitly reinstated or restored passenger service to a route where local passenger service had previously ended, which one's previous local passenger service ended in the earliest year? Tie-breaker: the line located in the United Kingdom.
expected_behavior: Camp Hill line — local passenger services ended in 1941 (confirmed permanently closed 1946); reinstated April 7, 2026 (~84 years later). The Lausanne tramway's predecessor closed in 1964 (later than 1941). Tie-breaker (UK location) confirms Camp Hill, located in Birmingham, England.

question: A 2026 light rail transit line in Toronto used four tunnel boring machines named Dennis, Lea, Don, and Humber. Two of those names — Dennis and Lea — together pay tribute to a specific Canadian poet. According to the Wikipedia article on that POET (NOT the rail line article), what is the poet's birth year and what genre is the poet most associated with?
expected_behavior: The line is Line 5 Eglinton; the TBMs Dennis and Lea were named in reference to Canadian poet Dennis Lee. Per the Wikipedia article on Dennis Lee: born August 31, 1939; best known for his children's poetry (notably 'Alligator Pie', 1974), though he also writes adult poetry and song lyrics. The agent must fetch the 'Dennis Lee (poet)' article to confirm the birth year and genre.

question: Among the 2026-opened railway/transit lines documented on Wikipedia, what is the difference in total stations between the longest line by number of stations and the shortest line by number of stations? Identify both lines.
expected_behavior: Line 6 Ningbo Rail Transit has 23 stations (longest by station count among lines documented). Line 5 Eglinton has 25 stations. Red Line (Mumbai) has 22 stations. GRU Airport People Mover has 4 stations (shortest). Difference between Line 5 Eglinton (25) and GRU People Mover (4) is 21 stations. The agent must compare counts across articles.

question: Which 2026 metro line in Paris, France opened on February 14, 2026 with a main span of 39.6 km?
expected_behavior: False premise — no Paris metro line matches the description. The 39.6 km figure comes from Line 6 of Ningbo Rail Transit (China), opened January 16, 2026. The February 14, 2026 date matches the Jinhua–Jiande high-speed railway (Zhejiang, China; 64.5 km). Neither is in Paris and there is no 2026 Paris metro line of that length. The agent should correct rather than invent.

question: Which 2026 high-speed railway line in mainland China has a maximum speed of 350 km/h and is over 200 kilometres long?
expected_behavior: False premise — the only 2026-opened high-speed railway in mainland China documented is the Jinhua–Jiande high-speed railway, which has a maximum speed of 250 km/h (NOT 350 km/h) and is 64.5 km long (NOT over 200 km). Agent should correct rather than invent.

question: Which 2026-opened railway line in Birmingham, England, primarily used by freight trains and some longer-distance passenger trains for over 80 years before reopening, sits between a station whose name is a compass direction (Kings Norton) and the city's primary central railway station? Tie-breaker: ELR designation 'St Andrews Junction to Kings Norton line.'
expected_behavior: Camp Hill line, between Kings Norton and Birmingham New Street. ELR designation: St Andrews Junction to Kings Norton line. Reopened April 7, 2026 with three new/restored stations (Moseley Village, Kings Heath, Pineapple Road).

question: Among 2026-opened metro / monorail / tramway / LRT / commuter rail lines (excluding high-speed intercity rail) documented on Wikipedia, which has the highest count of stations? Tie-breaker: the line that uses standard gauge while older heavy-rail lines in the same city use a different broader gauge.
expected_behavior: Line 5 Eglinton at 25 stations. Tie-breaker is satisfied — Line 5 Eglinton uses standard gauge while the older heavy-rail Toronto subway lines use the broad 'Toronto gauge.' Ningbo Line 6 has 23 stations; Red Line (Mumbai) has 22. Line 5 Eglinton wins on both counts.

question: Among the 2026-opened railway / transit lines documented on Wikipedia in this set, identify the line whose construction began earliest and the line whose construction began latest, and state both construction-start dates. Tie-breaker: only count lines whose Wikipedia article gives an explicit construction-start date.
expected_behavior: Earliest: Line 5 Eglinton — first phase construction began in 2011 (specifically tunnelling commenced after July 2010 TBM order; construction officially in 2011). Latest: Line 6 Ningbo Rail Transit — construction started December 28, 2022. Other documented dates: Jinhua–Jiande HSR officially began January 7, 2020; Line S2 (Ma'anshan section) started December 30, 2020 (Nanjing section December 28, 2021). Line 5 Eglinton is the clear earliest; Line 6 Ningbo is the clear latest. The agent must compare construction-start dates across articles.
